sections.paragraph:
περιάπτω 1 aor. 3 pl. περιῆψαν 3 Macc 3:7; ptc. περιάψας; pf. pass. inf. περιῆφθαι (EpArist 159) (Pind. et al.; PTebt 735, 11 [II b.c.]; TestSol P [PVindob 18, 37]; Philo; Jos., Ant. 12, 260) kindle πῦρ (Phalaris, Ep. 122, 2 Herch. v.l.) Lk 22:55.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural

ptc. ptc. = participle

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

inf. inf. = infinitive

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Pind Pind , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Phalaris Phalaris , tyrant of Acragas (VI b.c.), in whose name letters were written centuries later, II? a.d.—List 5

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 799
